Container.Name: Matrix
Container.Overview:
Matrix All-in-One is a plug-and-play Docker image for running your own Matrix homeserver on Unraid. It combines all the necessary components in a single container:
• Synapse (Matrix homeserver, based on the official Element image) • coturn (TURN/STUN server for voice and video calls) • Element Web (modern Matrix client, available at /element/) • Synapse-Admin (administration interface, available at /admin/) • lighttpd (lightweight web server for the static web UIs)
Plug-and-play: simply enter your SERVER_NAME and PostgreSQL connection details — on first run the container automatically generates the complete homeserver.yaml, configures coturn with a random TURN shared secret, and sets up Element Web. No SSH, no manual config file editing required.
IMPORTANT — Prerequisites: 1. A running PostgreSQL container (e.g. PostgreSQL15 from Community Applications). The database MUST be created with ENCODING UTF8 and LC_COLLATE='C' (required by Synapse). 2. A reverse proxy (e.g. Nginx Proxy Manager) for HTTPS termination on port 8008. 3. A domain pointing to your Unraid IP.
Step-by-step guide (PostgreSQL setup, NPM configuration, federation, creating the first user): https://github.com/junkerderprovinz/matrix#readme
Available after startup at: http://[IP]:[PORT:8080]/element/ – Element Web (Matrix client) http://[IP]:[PORT:8080]/admin/ – Synapse-Admin
Container.Requires: PostgreSQL container with a database: ENCODING UTF8, LC_COLLATE=C, LC_CTYPE=C

Config.Name: Matrix HTTP API
Config.Description: Synapse Matrix HTTP API. Must be exposed via a reverse proxy (e.g. Nginx Proxy Manager) with HTTPS. Do not expose directly to the internet.

Config.Name: Element Web + Synapse-Admin
Config.Description: lighttpd web server: Element Web at /element/ and Synapse-Admin at /admin/. Can be accessed directly on the LAN or exposed via NPM.

Config.Name: TURN TCP
Config.Description: coturn TURN/STUN over TCP. Required for voice and video calls. This port must be opened in your router/firewall.

Config.Name: TURN UDP
Config.Description: coturn TURN/STUN over UDP. Required for voice and video calls. This port must be opened in your router/firewall.

Config.Name: TURN TLS TCP
Config.Description: coturn TURN over TLS (TCP). Optional — only needed if TLS certificates are configured for coturn.

Config.Name: TURN TLS UDP
Config.Description: coturn TURN over TLS (UDP). Optional — only needed if TLS certificates are configured for coturn.

Config.Name: Prometheus Metrics
Config.Description: Prometheus metrics endpoint at /_synapse/metrics. Bind to a private network only — these metrics expose internal Synapse state.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Persistent data directory. Contains homeserver.yaml, media files, signing keys, and database connection info. NEVER delete this — doing so will destroy all signing keys and break federation.

Config.Name: TURN-TLS Certs
Config.Description: Optional. Mount your TLS cert directory here (must contain fullchain.pem and privkey.pem) to enable TURN over TLS on port 5349. If left empty, plain TURN on port 3478 still works.

Config.Name: SERVER_NAME
Config.Description: The Matrix domain of your homeserver, e.g. matrix.yourdomain.tld. This domain is used for Matrix IDs (@user:matrix.yourdomain.tld) and CANNOT BE CHANGED LATER without dropping the database.

Config.Name: REPORT_STATS
Config.Description: Send anonymous usage statistics to Matrix.org? 'yes' or 'no'. Recommended: 'no' for home servers.

Config.Name: POSTGRES_HOST
Config.Description: IP address of your PostgreSQL server. Use the Unraid host IP (e.g. 192.168.1.10) plus the host port your Postgres container exposes. Container names work too, but only when both containers share the same custom Docker network — IP+port is more reliable.

Config.Name: POSTGRES_USER
Config.Description: PostgreSQL username for Synapse. Must already exist in PostgreSQL (see README — Setting Up PostgreSQL).

Config.Name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Password for the PostgreSQL Synapse user. Treated securely as a Docker secret.

Config.Name: POSTGRES_DB
Config.Description: Name of the PostgreSQL database for Synapse. Must have been created with ENCODING UTF8, LC_COLLATE='C', LC_CTYPE='C' — otherwise Synapse will not start.

Config.Name: POSTGRES_PORT
Config.Description: PostgreSQL port. Default is 5432. Only change this if PostgreSQL is running on a different port.

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Container timezone, e.g. Europe/Vienna, Europe/Berlin, Europe/Zurich. Affects timestamps in logs.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID that Synapse runs as. Default: 99 (nobody on Unraid). Only change this if you encounter permission issues.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID that Synapse runs as. Default: 100 (users on Unraid). Only change this if you encounter permission issues.

Config.Name: ADMIN_USER
Config.Description: Optional. Localpart of an admin account to auto-create on first boot, e.g. 'admin' becomes @admin:SERVER_NAME. Only used once — leave empty after the user has been created. Requires ADMIN_PASSWORD to be set as well.

Config.Name: ADMIN_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Optional. Password for the auto-created admin account. Only used in combination with ADMIN_USER. After the admin user has been created on first boot you can safely clear this value.
